context: Checkpoint update 2026-04-29T23:20Z after CI recovery. Development resumed after compaction. Latest lane remains generic StreamChunk observations + AgentResponse projection. Host-owned credentials and opt-in LLM streaming boundary preserved.
current_tasks: CI run 25138262304 for d2205ae0 failed in @refarm.dev/tractor-rs#test:unit. Root failing test was host::wasi_bridge::tests::complete_http_stream_stores_first_chunk_before_response_closes with ureq/stable chunked transfer decoding error. Other route tests failed only because ENV_LOCK was poisoned after the first panic.

Failure reproduced locally with the closest safe command: cd packages/tractor && RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN=stable cargo test --lib complete_http_stream_stores_first_chunk_before_response_closes -- --nocapture. Non-stable/default local toolchain did not reproduce, stable did.

Fix committed locally: cf42c0a0 test(tractor): use content-length drip stream fixture. The drip SSE test now uses content-length response framing while still flushing first bytes before releasing the tail, preserving the incremental persistence assertion without relying on chunked decoding behavior.
Additional clippy cleanup committed locally: e95387e1 test(tractor): initialize stream assembly in one expression. This fixed a clippy warning surfaced while validating recent stream tests.

Local validation after fixes passed: RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN=stable cargo test --lib --quiet in packages/tractor; npm --prefix packages/tractor run lint; npm run agent:streaming:check; npm run agent:streaming:harness; npm run agent:streaming:clients; npm run project:validate --silent; git diff --check.

Important process note: do not use GitHub Actions as the test platform. Before pushing again, reproduce failures locally with closest safe command. Full cargo test remains avoided locally because AGENTS.md warns against unscoped cargo test in this 8GB environment; stable --lib matched the failing CI target without triggering the OOM risk.
blockers: Disk still tight: around 4.0GB free after local validations. Avoid global builds. Use npm run clean:light at checkpoints if needed.
Current local HEAD includes handoff/update/fix commits not yet pushed at the moment this handoff update is written. Check git status and log before push.

Broad clippy --tests was intentionally not used as a gate: it surfaces existing await_holding_lock debt in packages/tractor/tests/pi_agent_harness.rs unrelated to this slice. The package lint script uses cargo clippy without --tests and passes.
pi-lens/LSP monitor continues crashing on Rust/Markdown writes with memory access out of bounds.
next_actions: If working tree is clean, push develop only after confirming the local validation list remains green.
After push, use gh run watch --exit-status only as final confirmation, not as exploratory testing. If it fails, inspect logs and reproduce locally before another push.
Next technical slices after CI recovery: introduce StreamSession node/builder, or add generic StreamChunk query/watch ergonomics. Keep slices atomic.
If touching streaming tests again, run stable cargo test --lib for tractor before push.

Should generic StreamChunk nodes be exposed through a CLI/watch API before deeper provider parser extraction?
Should final markers exist for tool-call-only streams as distinct payload kinds?
Should env-only LLM_STREAM_RESPONSES be promoted into governed config in a later ADR/task?
